description: Interpreting a pipeline-induced distribution shift as genuine drift and retraining, baking the bug into the model.
body: Failure mode: a join change, unit error, or partial load shifts a feature distribution, drift monitoring flags it, and the team retrains on the corrupted data, encoding the bug into the new model and masking the real defect. Detection signals: a sharp distribution cliff coinciding with a deploy; row counts or null rates also moved; the shift reverses when the suspected bug is fixed. Do not retrain before ruling out a data defect. Safer fallback: run a pipeline-integrity check (counts, joins, units) against a baseline first, fix the source and backfill, then reassess whether real drift remains.
